evidence_items.locator: Table II (context: in vitro permeation narrative)
evidence_items.snippet: Light microscope (left) and scanning electron microscope (right) images of the seven MN array designs prepared with dissolving formulation (a, D1 to f, D6). Pharm Res (2020) 37: 174 Page 9 of 15 174 Ibuprofen sodium (IBU Na) used as model drug in IVPT through dermatomed porcine skin using modified Franz diffusion cells with HFMN arrays. Quantitative permeation endpoint data (flux, cumulative permeation) not present in supplied pages; likely in Results figures/tables on later pages not provided. Cabotegravir sodium

provenance.route_notes: Comparative IVPT study using Franz cells with human epidermis, porcine full-thickness skin, silicone membrane, and PAMPA model. Four ibuprofen formulations (IBUGEL, IBULEVE spray, PG solution, PEG 300 solution) all at 5% w/w. Finite dose conditions. Permeation up to 48h for skin, 6h for silicone/PAMPA. Cumulative permeation data in figures; percentage permeated in Figure 2. Dose 3.6-4 µL per Franz cell (~1 cm2 area).

route_raw_labels.formulation_carrier_snippet: Table 1: Excipients included in commercial and experimental formulations and proposed functions. Commercial formulations: IBUGEL (Ibuprofen 5% w/w) and IBULEVE Speed Relief 5% Spray; experimental: 5% w/w ibuprofen in IPA/PEG 300 or IPA/PG.
route_raw_labels.notes: Comparative IVPT study using Franz cells with human epidermis, porcine full-thickness skin, silicone membrane, and PAMPA model. Four ibuprofen formulations (IBUGEL, IBULEVE spray, PG solution, PEG 300 solution) all at 5% w/w. Finite dose conditions. Permeation up to 48h for skin, 6h for silicone/PAMPA. Cumulative permeation data in figures; percentage permeated in Figure 2. Dose 3.6-4 µL per Franz cell (~1 cm2 area).

source_notes: Range of human skin cumulative permeation at 48 h across all formulations: 11–38 µg/cm²; individual formulation values not disaggregated in text.

barrier: human skin (heat-separated epidermis), full-thickness porcine ear skin, silicone membrane (250 µm), Skin PAMPA membrane

route_raw_labels.notes: Comparative IVPT study using Franz cells with human epidermis, porcine full-thickness skin, silicone membrane, and PAMPA model. Four ibuprofen formulations (IBUGEL, IBULEVE spray, PG solution, PEG 300 solution) all at 5% w/w. Finite dose conditions. Permeation up to 48h for skin, 6h for silicone/PAMPA. Cumulative permeation data in figures; percentage permeated in Figure 2. Dose 3.6-4 µL per Franz cell (~1 cm2 area).

ot diffuse from the colloids forming under UV light and suggesting stable micelle structures (Figure S36). Formulation compositions (particle size, TPGS%, HPMC%) detailed in Tables I and II. 2³ factorial design with 8 formulations (F1–F8). No supplementary material referenced.

Formulations were added to the donor compartment as an infinite dose - for the gel formulations was determined using Fick’s law J = P × Cd − Cr . 1 where, J is the flux per unit area, P is the permeability coefficient, and Cd and Cr are the concentrations of drug in the donor and receptor solutions, respectively.

evidence_items.snippet: ; accepted 12 July 2019; published online 24 July 2019 Abstract. The humidity was a well-known method to hydrate the skin; however, the published data were varied, and systemic experiments in the previous papers were few. Therefore, the in vitro permeation of excised porcine ear skin by drugs with different polarities [aminopyrine (AMP), antipyrine (ANP), methylparaben (MP), and ibuprofen (IP)] was analyzed under a constant skin surface temperature with different temperatures and humidities to reveal the effects of

ed 12 July 2019; published online 24 July 2019 Abstract. The humidity was a well-known method to hydrate the skin; however, the published data were varied, and systemic experiments in the previous papers were few. Therefore, the in vitro permeation of excised porcine ear skin by drugs with different polarities [aminopyrine (AMP), antipyrine (ANP), methylparaben (MP), and ibuprofen (IP)] was analyzed under a constant skin surface temperature with different temperatures and humidities to reveal the effects of tempera

increasing the relative humidity (RH) from 28 to 70% at 27°C led to a signiﬁcant increase in + percutaneous absorption of caffeine (8). These analyses were performed using devices that control humidity and skin surface temperature on the donor side while the receptor ﬂuid temperature was maintained at 37°C. Ishida et al. used a CO2 chamber to show that the effect of humidity on the in vitro penetration of human skin by drugs increased the permeability coefﬁcient (P) values of nicotine and isosorbide dinitrate at 9

increasing the relative humidity (RH) from 28 to 70% at 27°C led to a signiﬁcant increase in + percutaneous absorption of caffeine (8). These analyses were performed using devices that control humidity and skin surface temperature on the donor side while the receptor ﬂuid temperature was maintained at 37°C. Ishida et al. used a CO2 chamber to show that the effect of humidity on the in vitro penetration of human skin by drugs increased the permeability coefﬁcient (P) values of nicotine and isosorbide dinitrate at 9

increasing the relative humidity (RH) from 28 to 70% at 27°C led to a signiﬁcant increase in + percutaneous absorption of caffeine (8). Ishida et al. used a CO2 chamber to show that the effect of humidity on the in vitro penetration of human skin by drugs increased the permeability coefﬁcient (P) values of nicotine and isosorbide dinitrate at 9

solution for each model drug was stirred for 1-h at 40°C or 27°C according to the skin surface temperature before conducting the permeation experiment. The diffusion cell was equilibrated for 1-h for each environmental condi- tion, followed by the addition of 1.0 ml of saturated donor solution to each donor cell. During the permeation study, 100 μl of receiver ﬂuid was withdrawn every 1-h, and an equal volume of fresh PBS was added immediately. The receiver samples were analyzed using an HPLC system, as described b

provenance.route_notes: Ibuprofen (IP, log Ko/w 3.94) is one of four model drugs. Saturated donor solutions used. Franz diffusion cell, porcine ear skin, 8-hour experiment. Endpoints include Jss, P, tlag, KL, DL-2 under 4 temperature/humidity conditions. Permeability enhancement ratio (ER) for IP at T48RH95, T50RH55, T52RH30 are 1.28, 1.38, 1.34 respectively vs T27RH55 baseline.

ed 12 July 2019; published online 24 July 2019 Abstract. The humidity was a well-known method to hydrate the skin; however, the published data were varied, and systemic experiments in the previous papers were few. Therefore, the in vitro permeation of excised porcine ear skin by drugs with different polarities [aminopyrine (AMP), antipyrine (ANP), methylparaben (MP), and ibuprofen (IP)] was analyzed under a constant skin surface temperature with different temperatures and humidities to reveal the effects of tempera

increasing the relative humidity (RH) from 28 to 70% at 27°C led to a signiﬁcant increase in + percutaneous absorption of caffeine (8). provenance.route_notes: IVRT study using a horizontal two-chamber diffusion cell (not Franz) with a 75 µm silicone membrane as synthetic barrier. Ibuprofen used as the anionic component of an ionic liquid (IL) paired with lidocaine cation. Endpoint is steady-state flux (µmol/cm²/h) over 1–8 h. No skin barrier used; purely synthetic membrane permeation to assess thermodynamic activity. Not a topical ibuprofen formulation study per se, but ibuprofen permeation data through silicone membrane are reported.

route_raw_labels.notes: IVRT study using a horizontal two-chamber diffusion cell (not Franz) with a 75 µm silicone membrane as synthetic barrier. Ibuprofen used as the anionic component of an ionic liquid (IL) paired with lidocaine cation. Endpoint is steady-state flux (µmol/cm²/h) over 1–8 h. No skin barrier used; purely synthetic membrane permeation to assess thermodynamic activity. Not a topical ibuprofen formulation study per se, but ibuprofen permeation data through silicone membrane are reported.

source_notes: Preparation #0 is ibuprofen only (not an IL preparation). Suspended IBU = 1.0 molar fraction.

provenance.route_notes: IVRT study using a horizontal two-chamber diffusion cell (not Franz) with a 75 µm silicone membrane as synthetic barrier. Ibuprofen used as the anionic component of an ionic liquid (IL) paired with lidocaine cation. Endpoint is steady-state flux (µmol/cm²/h) over 1–8 h. No skin barrier used; purely synthetic membrane permeation to assess thermodynamic activity. Not a topical ibuprofen formulation study per se, but ibuprofen permeation data through silicone membrane are reported.

LBG (cp = 0.5% w/V) was dispersed in distilled water, under magnetic and mechanical stirring, at 80° C for 24 h and at room temperature for 24 additional hours (37). The solutions were then exhaustively dialyzed at 4° C against distilled water, using dialysis membranes with a cut-off 12,000–14,000. In order to convert the xanth polymer into the sodium form, NaOH 0.2 N was added to the dialyzed solution up to pH = 7.0. Finally, the samples were freeze-dried and stored in a desiccator until use.
